title: CFAs: Deployment and Management of the Oky App in Zambia
funding_organization: United Nations International Children’s Emergency Fund (UNICEF)
description: The United Nations International Children’s Emergency Fund is inviting expressions of interest for the adaptation, deployment, and management of the Oky app in Zambia, with a specific focus on child protection and empowering adolescent girls. The initiative targets …
relevance_reasoning: Meets all minimum requirements (nonprofits eligible, not a competition, focuses on children, Zambia in Sub-Saharan Africa, relates to technology for adolescents), but requires adaptation: Zambia-specific app deployment vs. Kenya-focused hardware for schools.
how_it_helps: Not applicable - grant is for Oky app management in Zambia, not IT hardware distribution.

title: RFPs: NGO Programs Benefitting Refugees 2025 (Ethiopia, Kenya, South Sudan)
funding_organization: Bureau of Population, Refugees and Migration (PRM)
description: The Bureau of Population, Refugees and Migration (PRM) seeks concept notes focusing on various sectors such as education, health, protection and WASH. Program Area Proposed program concept notes must align with one or more of the following program areas: …
relevance_reasoning: Meets all minimum requirements (NGOs accepted, not a contest, education focus including children/refugees, Kenya included, relates to technology education/digital literacy via education sector proposals). Good match but requires adaptation to refugee beneficiaries.
how_it_helps: Submit concept note proposing refurbishment and distribution of IT equipment to schools serving refugee children in Kenya, with digital literacy and technology education programs.

title: UNICEF seeking the Next Blue Unicorn EdTech Tools to Bridge Global Learning Gaps
description: The UNICEF Global Learning Innovation Hub has announced a groundbreaking portfolio initiative called the "Blue Unicorn Farm," which seeks innovative EdTech solutions to address the global learning crisis.
relevance_reasoning: Meets all minimum requirements: open to nonprofits (eligibility none), not a competition (portfolio initiative), focuses on education/learning for children/students, global scope includes developing countries/Africa, relates to digital literacy/technology education via EdTech. Strong but not perfect as not hardware-specific or Africa-exclusive.
how_it_helps: Prepare and submit an application positioning refurbished IT equipment as an innovative EdTech solution for bridging digital gaps in underserved African schools, emphasizing circular economy and digital literacy impact.
